title: Goniophlebium hendersonii
taxonomy: Goniophlebium hendersonii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Goniophlebium and is a type of epiphytic fern. The classification of Goniophlebium hendersonii also includes the rank of species, which is a taxonomic category below the genus. The family Polypodiaceae is a group of vascular plants that are commonly known as polypodioid ferns. Goniophlebium hendersonii is native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands.

intro: Goniophlebium hendersonii, also known as Henderson's Goniophlebium or Tawa, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Goniophlebium hendersonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Goniophlebium hendersonii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which is used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
